body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full
driver_capability: ready

## Scope: text-classifier path, NOT the structured-tool path

`agent-question-pending` is the **trailing-`?` text-classifier** scenario, distinct from `user-blocking-question` even though `scenarios.json` maps both to the same `coverage_id: user-blocking-question`. Per the `scenarios.json` description, this cell "Validates the trailing-? path through the state classifier (session.IsWaitingForUserInput → state_classifier.go decision 2a): at turn end the session transitions working→waiting instead of working→ready because the last assistant message ends in `?`. This is the text classifier path, not a structured AskUserQuestion tool." `verify` is `{has_waiting_state: true, final_state: waiting}`; `requires: [headless_mode]`.

The existing coverage cell `user-blocking-question/aider = partial / daemon:incapable / driver:ready` ("can ask via /ask interactively but no JSONL-style tool marker; not detected") is the verdict for the **structured AskUserQuestion tool** primitive — aider genuinely lacks that, and the daemon is `incapable` of observing a tool marker that never gets written. It does NOT describe the trailing-`?` text path this scenario exercises, which is a different mechanism with a different verdict. That split is exactly the #476 separation: the structured-tool cell is `incapable` (no trace in the Source), this text-path cell is `full` (the `?` survives into `LastAssistantText` and the classifier acts on it).

## agent_supports: yes

Aider is an LLM-driven REPL. Producing an assistant turn whose final sentence ends in `?` is exactly what the recipe asks for, and aider also ships a first-class `/ask` chat mode (`/ask <msg>`, `/chat-mode ask`, `--chat-mode ask`) for question/clarification turns that make no code edits. There is no structured "question tool" — the question is plain prose printed to the terminal and appended to the per-project markdown chat log `.aider.chat.history.md`. For the trailing-`?` primitive (assistant prose ending in `?`), prose support is precisely what's required, so `yes`.

## daemon_capability: full

The aider adapter uses `agent.FilesUnderCWD{Filename: ".aider.chat.history.md", Parser: RawLineParser{...}}` (agent.go) — append-only markdown per project CWD, tailed line-by-line through `ParseLineRaw`. A trace exists in the watched Source AND the parser/classifier handle it today, so this is `full`, not `bug`/`incapable`:

1. `#### <prompt>` → `user_message` with `ClearToolNames: true`. The tailer clears `lastAssistantText` (tailer.go ~890) and the session goes `working`.
2. Assistant prose (ending in `?`) is buffered between the `####` line and the next `> Tokens:` line (parser.go `ParseLineRaw` prose branch).
3. `> Tokens: …` → `closeModelCall` emits an `assistant_message` whose `AssistantText` carries the buffered prose **with the trailing `?` preserved**. The tailer copies a non-empty `AssistantText` into `lastAssistantText` → `session.LastAssistantText` (tailer.go 886-887; tailer_metrics.go 387). This contract is explicitly pinned by `TestParser_TrailingQuestionMark_PreservedForWaitingClassification` in parser_test.go, which asserts the emitted `assistant_message.AssistantText` ends in `?` precisely "because the tailer feeds assistant_message.AssistantText into LastAssistantText, which session.IsWaitingForUserInput inspects."
4. After ~1500ms quiet, `IdleFlush` synthesizes `turn_done` with an **empty** `AssistantText` (parser.go 216-229). Empty `AssistantText` does NOT overwrite `lastAssistantText`, and the synthetic `turn_done` does NOT set `ClearToolNames`, so the stored question text survives (tailer.go 886-891).
5. On that `turn_done`, `IsAgentDone()` is true, so `ClassifyState` reaches decision 2a. `IsWaitingForUserInput()` → `ExtractQuestionSnippet(LastAssistantText)` finds the trailing `?` (session.go 237-244, 263-286) and returns the `working → waiting` transition (state_classifier.go 47-56). Final state `waiting`, with a visible `waiting` episode — matching the scenario's `verify` block.

This is the canonical text-classifier path; the daemon emits the events the spec asserts AS IT EXISTS TODAY. Hence `full`, not the structured-tool `incapable`.

## driver_capability: ready

The interactive driver (`.claude/skills/ir:onboard-agent/scripts/drive-aider-interactive.sh`) implements `send`/`slash`, `wait_turn`, `interrupt`, and `sleep` (the `case "$type"` block). The recipe script for this cell uses only `send`, `wait_turn`, and `sleep` — every step type it needs. Teardown is the driver's built-in `tmux kill-session` at script end (deliberately NOT Ctrl-D / `/exit`), so the post-turn `waiting` dwell survives until the single `process_exited`; no driver primitive is missing. Hence `ready`, not `gap:*`.

## Primitive / capability anchor

Primitive exercised (verbatim from scenario-meanings `### user-blocking-question`): `tool_calls` + AskUserQuestion (or adapter equivalent) — agent can pause mid-turn and await structured user input via a dedicated question tool call. For aider the "adapter equivalent" of pausing-for-input is the trailing-`?` text cue rather than a tool call. The closest capability key in `replaydata/agents/aider/capabilities.json` is `tool_calls: true` (confirmed present and true); aider has no structured question tool (no `AskUserQuestion`/`question` entry exists in the features map), which is why `slash_commands: true` (`/ask`) plus the text-classifier path — not a tool marker — is what makes this cell observable.

## route

agent_supports: yes + daemon_capability: full + driver_capability: ready → **record**. Stage 2 (recipe) can proceed; the recording should show `ready → working → waiting` and stop in `waiting`.
caveats: feature-invisible-but-spec-compliant (inverted): aider has NO structured AskUserQuestion/question tool — the `partial / daemon:incapable` in the user-blocking-question coverage cell is correct for that structured-tool primitive. This cell is `full` only because it targets the trailing-`?` TEXT path, a different mechanism. Don't conflate the two.

metric-drift-downstream: the working→waiting transition is gated on the ~1500ms IdleFlush turn_done (parser.go aiderIdleTurnDoneAfter) AND the daemon's SessionDetector working-refresh tick (~5s). The waiting episode is real but lands a few seconds after the last transcript line, not instantly at end-of-prose; the recipe's 8s trailing sleep covers that latency.

Detection keys on LastAssistantText, truncated to the last ~200 chars (parser.go truncate). A reply whose `?` falls outside the trailing 200-char window after truncation could be missed; the recipe ('one short clarifying question') keeps it well inside the window.

Heuristic edges: ExtractQuestionSnippet skips rhetorical Q&A pairs and requires the `?` to be followed by whitespace/markdown-wrapper/end-of-string. A plain single-question reply per the recipe is squarely detected (cf. parser_test + session_test cases).

End-of-turn is always synthesized by the 1500ms IdleFlush (never an in-band end marker); a >1500ms inter-call silence inside a multi-call turn could synthesize a premature turn_done. The single-prose-block recipe avoids this.

sources.note: Source = FilesUnderCWD{.aider.chat.history.md, RawLineParser} — markdown transcript tailed via ParseLineRaw.
closeModelCall emits assistant_message preserving prose (incl. trailing ?); IdleFlush emits turn_done with empty AssistantText; truncate caps at 200 chars.
TestParser_TrailingQuestionMark_PreservedForWaitingClassification pins the daemon_capability:full contract: assistant_message.AssistantText ends in '?' so session.IsWaitingForUserInput detects it.
Lines ~886-891: lastAssistantText updated only on non-empty AssistantText, cleared only on ClearToolNames — so the empty-text idle-flush turn_done preserves the question text.
Decision 2a: IsAgentDone + IsWaitingForUserInput → working→waiting (the trailing-? text path this scenario validates) — the classifier handles the trace today, hence daemon:full.
IsWaitingForUserInput → ExtractQuestionSnippet over LastAssistantText (literal trailing '?'); rhetorical/URL exclusions.
driver_capability:ready — case block implements send/slash, wait_turn, interrupt, sleep; recipe uses only send/wait_turn/sleep; teardown is built-in tmux kill-session, no missing primitive.
agent-question-pending entry: trailing-? text-classifier path, NOT structured AskUserQuestion; verify {has_waiting_state, final_state: waiting}; recipe script uses send/wait_turn/sleep only.
Existing user-blocking-question/aider cell = partial / daemon:incapable / driver:ready — verdict for the STRUCTURED question tool, distinct from this text-path scenario (which is daemon:full).
tool_calls:true (Primitive-exercised anchor), slash_commands:true (/ask), headless_mode:true; no structured question tool flag — the /ask + trailing-? text path is what makes this cell observable.
Aider /ask chat mode: ask questions / discuss a plan without editing code; question turns are prose, not a structured tool call.
Confirms .aider.chat.history.md is the per-project markdown chat log the adapter tails.
